results.rendered_html: ātivāhika (von ativāha) adj. Bez. des feinen Körpers (liṅgaśarīra). der die Seele in eine fernere Geburt hinüberführt (schneller als der Wind  COLEBR. )  BRAHMAS. 4,4,8 . ātivāhika eko 'sti deho 'nyastvādhibhautikaḥ Cit. beim Schol. zu  KAP. 3,11 . sūkṣmaśarīra  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. I, 245  (ati˚).  WILSON, SĀṂKHYAK. S. 133 . Schol. zu  ĀŚV. GṚHY. 4,4,8 .

results.rendered_html:
AtivAhakaAtivAhika a. [ativāhe niyuktaḥ ṭhak] Employed to convey to the other world; ātivāhikāstalligāt  Br. Sūt. 
— kaṃ An epithet of the Sūkṣma
(pb) Śarīra (in Sān. phil.) from its surpassing the wind in swiftness.
